metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
As an Account Development Specialist (End-User), you will be on the front lines of customer engagement — identifying, contacting, and selling to new and existing end-user customers and regionally based Engineering firms who could benefit from our products or services. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on direct outreach, enjoys helping people solve problems, and wants to build foundational sales skills in a fast-paced environment. This role is responsible for driving sales of all relevant end-user-focused products by combining a blend of technical knowledge, strategic thinking, and exceptional communication skills. The Account Development Specialist works closely with our Channel and Field Application Engineering teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets.  This role plays a pivotal part in the development of accounts, with the goal of crossing a threshold to where developed accounts are then assigned to Sales Engineers. This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Meet or exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President—Field Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Generate leads through cold calls, emails, and networking events.
•	Support the preparation of proposals, presentations, and sales materials
•	Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Collaborate with the entire Phoenix Contact team to align efforts, effectively communicate both verbally and written.
•	Track and report on business development activities and performance metrics.
•	Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
•	Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 20 sales calls per week
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Work closely with Harrisburg Product teams to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within the area of focus.
•	Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
•	Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned distributor locations.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
•	Qualify and follow up on all leads within the assigned territory.
•	Follow up on all quotations within the assigned territory for the given account portfolio.
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year degree preferred.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
•	1 to 3 years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Comfortable making cold calls and sending outreach emails.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 



ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 35-40% or as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance (Must Be Included as Worded Here by Legal).

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Summary

This is a hands-on, activity-driven role ideal for someone who is eager to learn, contribute, and grow within a dynamic team responsible for driving sales of all Phoenix Contact products by combining engineering knowledge with strong interpersonal and business skills.  The Account Development Specialist (OEM) role plays a key role in identifying growth opportunities, building relationships with potential and existing developing customers, and supporting the sales pipeline through consistent outreach and follow-up. This role involves identifying customer needs, recommending suitable products or solutions, and providing technical support throughout the sales process. The Account Development Specialist works closely with our Channel and Field Application Engineering teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets.  This role plays a pivotal part in the development of accounts, with the goal of crossing a threshold to where developed accounts are then assigned to Sales Engineers.

The territory for this role is Ohio, Kentucky, Tennessee

Responsibilities

Meet or exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President—Field Sales.
Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
Generate leads through cold calls, emails, and networking events.
Support the preparation of proposals, presentations, and sales materials
Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
Collaborate with the entire Phoenix Contact team to align efforts, effectively communicate both verbally and written.
Track and report on business development activities and performance metrics.
Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 20 sales calls per week
Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
Work closely with Harrisburg Product teams to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within the area of focus.
Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned distributor locations.
Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
Qualify and follow up on all leads within the assigned territory.
Follow up on all quotations within the assigned territory for the given account portfolio.
Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Four-year degree in technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
1 to 3 years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
Experience working with distribution preferred.
Comfortable making cold calls and sending outreach emails.
Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.

Essential Job Functions

Must be able to travel via airplane.
Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
Must be able to travel overnight 50+% or as dictated by assigned territory.
Comply with company dress code guidelines.
Punctuality is required.
Regular and on-time attendance

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS: 

This position is primarily responsible for supporting the preventative maintenance and basic hardware error troubleshooting of the automated storage and retrieval system (AS/RS). The AS/RS Technician I will be expected to read system schematics and collaborate with logistics personnel and Viastore hotline support to assist in resolving system errors. This role includes documenting maintenance activities, error resolutions, and work orders in the facility’s CMMS software system. The AS/RS Technician I may also assist with general facility maintenance tasks as prioritized by the Facilities Manager. All facilities personnel are expected to perform their duties in a manner that ensures the safety of themselves and all employees. Success in this role requires strong teamwork, attention to detail, and effective communication skills. 

 

COMPETENCY PROFILE:  

Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership 

 

RESPONSIBILITIES: 

Assist in the preventative maintenance of the distribution center’s AS/RS & conveyor equipment 

Perform routine scheduled work orders on various pieces of equipment throughout the logistics automation system  

Identify, troubleshoot & correct logistics system failures to support the warehouse team 

Work with OEM (Viastore & other sub-contractors) to troubleshoot and implement corrective actions to system errors 

Document and communicate the use of all spare parts for appropriate replenishment 

Maintain statistics & reports on system’s performance as needed 

Communicate with sub-contractors (suppliers) for system upgrades, failures, and / or repairs, as needed 

Provide awareness training of warehouse team leaders on system errors and troubleshooting of routine failures 

Responsible to maintain & update the preventative maintenance schedule in facilities CMMS software tool   

Participate in Logistics Planning shift meetings as needed 

Document “history logs” within the CMMS maintenance software for tracking work and add to the knowledge base for others’ awareness 

At times, perform general facilities maintenance tasks as time permits and prioritized by the facilities manager   

Follow all OSHA and company safety rules in all maintenance procedures 

Follow all environmental guidelines when using / disposing of materials 

  

QUALIFICATIONS:  

High School Diploma or GED. 

Five (3-5) years’ experience in large logistics facility maintenance operations. 

Ability to read and use engineering drawings. 

Good understanding of controls and automated systems 

Good decision-making skills. 

Computer experience in Microsoft Office preferred. 

Ability to prioritize workload. 

Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making.  

Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes.  

Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands.  

Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers.  

Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed.  

Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward.  

 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS: 

Must be able to stand for 8 hours a day. 

Must be able to lift 60 pounds. 

Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to use own vehicle for local trips to Home Depot, etc. (with reimbursement). 

Must be able to work flexible hours, including evenings, weekends, and PRN overtime. 

Must be willing to support the facility maintenance team in a collaborative environment. 

Must be able to handle extreme temperatures. 

Must be able to drive for up to 2 hours. 

Must be able to speak and understand English to communicate verbally and in writing. 

Regular and on-time attendance. 

Must be able to climb up to 50 feet

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:
Associate Product Support Specialists (APSS) provide level 1 technical support to Phoenix Contact customers, sales personnel and channel partners. The Associate Product Support Specialist will be responsible for providing technical advice and troubleshooting assistance for all Phoenix Contact products and related systems. The APSS will work with Phoenix Contact customers and channel partners and communicate all support case details to our customers and our sales team. While not supporting customers directly, the APSS will be expected to develop their knowledge of related products and systems.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Provide answers to questions through inbound calls, e-mails, and chats from customers and sales team on technical issues as related to level 1 support
• Document customer inquiries in CRM system to provide customer and sales team detailed summary of required actions needed to meet customer needs
• Continuously develop knowledge on Phoenix Contact products and their applications within relevant industries
• Work to continually improve the quality of service offered to our customers and partners
• Understand and maintain a working knowledge of any industry standards that play a role in industrial systems
• Be on call for emergency service when required

QUALIFICATIONS:
• Bachelor degree in engineering or technical focus preferred
• BSEE/EET/MFET/Computer Engineering, or related field
• Five years minimum industrial automation/electronics experience, with AS in Engineering or related field
• Knowledge of electrical engineering design fundamentals related to current, voltage, resistance, impedance, power dissipation, 4-20 mA current loops, single & three phase power 120/240/480VAC
• Ability to interpret electrical and mechanical schematics
• Ability to learn or knowledge of system components, standards, and best practices:
• Industrial Connection Technologies
• Panel installation, layout and marking
• Process system signal transmission and isolation
• Relay technology, and discrete signal transmission
• Power generation, transmission, distribution, and protection for AC and DC circuits
• Functional safety systems, specifications and standards
• Industrial controllers, distributed IO systems and communication protocols
• Network security, infrastructure and design as applied in industrial control systems
• Visualization technology and best practices
• Willingness and ability to adapt to changing departmental needs including but not limited to, schedule changes
• Excellent organizational, time management, customer service and problem-solving skills while working in an environment with frequent interruptions
• Ability to communicate technical concepts and principles to non-technical people orally and in writing
• Ability to analyze and anticipate situations, define problems and objectives, recognize alternatives, formulate solutions and implementation strategies
• Ability to exercise flexibility, initiative, sound judgment and discretion.
• Excellent oral/written communication skills
• Motivated self-starter with the ability to work autonomously and as part of a team
• Ability to set priorities and perform simultaneous tasks
• Ability to meet deadlines and adjust to changing priorities to meet business goals
• Proficient computer skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
• Effective interpersonal skills with comfort in delivering group presentations
• Must be willing to work any of the following shifts based on departmental needs
• Monday - Friday 8-5, with the possibility one day per week may have a 11-8 shift
• Monday - Thursday 9-6, and Friday 8-5
• Monday - Thursday 9-7, and Friday 8-12
• Displays proficiency in the following global competencies:
• Initiative – the understanding of what action needs to be taken and completing these tasks while seeking the perspective of others.
• Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility) – taking personal responsibility for mistakes; does what he/she commits to and is honest and forthright.
• Collaboration – works as a team player to make a difference within the business and expresses gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance, and support
• Willingness to Learn – handles change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and is open minded and curious to face new challenges.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
• 5% Travel Requirement (1% - 3% Overseas)
• Must be able to lift 50 pounds
• Must be able to sit and/or stand for extended periods of time (2-4 hours at a time)
• Must be able to work on a computer for extended periods of time (up to 4 hours at a time)
• Must be able to travel in vehicles (automobiles, trains, and planes) for up to 10 hours
• Must be able to communicate effectively in verbal and written English
• Must comply with company dress code guidelines
• Punctuality and attendance in accordance with company policies
• Timely reporting of expenses

metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:	
The (Business Development Manager – Segment Discipline – See Appendix) serves as a customer-facing salesperson  responsible for managing business development efforts engineered projects and applications specific to the sited discipline. This role is responsible for driving growth, supporting hub strategy execution, and collaborating with industry managers and regional teams. The ideal candidate has a background in engineering and sales, with experience in application-based selling and stakeholder engagement within the segment.  This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Deliver Strategies; Develop People and Teams; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Lead Change; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Manage business development efforts within the Applications & Engineered Projects Hub, ensuring alignment with segment and industry strategies.
•	Collaborate with engineering, product, and industry teams to develop tailored customer solutions and respond to market needs.
•	Utilize CRM and enablement tools to manage pursuits, track performance, and maintain visibility across opportunities.
•	Maintain accountability for KPIs and OKRs related to hub strategy execution and customer engagement.
•	Foster a collaborative and innovative team culture focused on excellence, knowledge sharing, and customer value.
•	Apply sales acumen across multiple disciplines—including Critical Infrastructure, Applications, and Engineered Projects—leveraging transferable skills to support solution development.
•	Collaborate with internal stakeholders to understand and adapt to discipline-specific requirements, including process-focused and infrastructure environments.
•	Align role execution with segment strategy as defined by Industry Managers, ensuring consistency with broader business objectives.
•	Technical acumen to serve as a subject matter expert within a focused segment area, contributing insights and guidance to strategic initiatives and customer solutions.
•	Support localization and execution of global segment strategies, maintaining awareness of leadership direction (e.g., German-led initiatives).
•	Demonstrate professional expertise in assigned segment, providing mentorship and technical leadership as needed.
•	Perform other duties as assigned by leadership to support evolving business needs.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	5–10 years of experience in business development, engineering projects, or technical sales.
•	Strong understanding of engineered solutions and application-based selling.
•	Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills.
•	Experience with CRM platforms and sales enablement tools.
•	Engineering discipline with sales experience preferred.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel by air
•	Occasional overseas travel for extended periods required
•	Occasional Domestic Travel required
•	Must be able to sit for extended periods of time
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in English
•	Must be able to lift 25 lbs.
•	Regular and on-time attendance (Must Be Included Was Worded Here by Legal).   
Experience sales professional working with low voltage technology in the Transmission and Distribution sector of the US National Electrical Grid.  Understanding of devices used within this area of focus specific to, but not limited in, high voltage equipment, application and technical theory behind need and deployment.  Understanding of how control and telemetry are deployed in this space.  Advanced understanding of devices and software tools to enhance both telemetry and control in the modernization efforts within the segment.

The Designer II is responsible for creating and sustaining products and solutions that meet customer and business requirements. SYNOPSIS:
Associate Product Support Specialists (APSS) provide level 1 technical support to Phoenix Contact customers, sales personnel and channel partners. The Associate Product Support Specialist will be responsible for providing technical advice and troubleshooting assistance for all Phoenix Contact products and related systems. The APSS will work with Phoenix Contact customers and channel partners and communicate all support case details to our customers and our sales team. While not supporting customers directly, the APSS will be expected to develop their knowledge of related products and systems.

RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Provide answers to questions through inbound calls, e-mails, and chats from customers and sales team on technical issues as related to level 1 support
• Document customer inquiries in CRM system to provide customer and sales team detailed summary of required actions needed to meet customer needs
• Continuously develop knowledge on Phoenix Contact products and their applications within relevant industries
• Work to continually improve the quality of service offered to our customers and partners
• Understand and maintain a working knowledge of any industry standards that play a role in industrial systems
• Be on call for emergency service when required

QUALIFICATIONS:
• Bachelor degree in engineering or technical focus preferred
• BSEE/EET/MFET/Computer Engineering, or related field
• Five years minimum industrial automation/electronics experience, with AS in Engineering or related field
• Knowledge of electrical engineering design fundamentals related to current, voltage, resistance, impedance, power dissipation, 4-20 mA current loops, single & three phase power 120/240/480VAC
• Ability to interpret electrical and mechanical schematics
• Ability to learn or knowledge of system components, standards, and best practices:
• Industrial Connection Technologies
• Panel installation, layout and marking
• Process system signal transmission and isolation
• Relay technology, and discrete signal transmission
• Power generation, transmission, distribution, and protection for AC and DC circuits
• Functional safety systems, specifications and standards
• Industrial controllers, distributed IO systems and communication protocols
• Network security, infrastructure and design as applied in industrial control systems
• Visualization technology and best practices
• Willingness and ability to adapt to changing departmental needs including but not limited to, schedule changes
• Excellent organizational, time management, customer service and problem-solving skills while working in an environment with frequent interruptions
• Ability to communicate technical concepts and principles to non-technical people orally and in writing
• Ability to analyze and anticipate situations, define problems and objectives, recognize alternatives, formulate solutions and implementation strategies
• Ability to exercise flexibility, initiative, sound judgment and discretion.
• Excellent oral/written communication skills
• Motivated self-starter with the ability to work autonomously and as part of a team
• Ability to set priorities and perform simultaneous tasks
• Ability to meet deadlines and adjust to changing priorities to meet business goals
• Proficient computer skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
• Effective interpersonal skills with comfort in delivering group presentations
• Must be willing to work any of the following shifts based on departmental needs
• Monday - Friday 8-5, with the possibility one day per week may have a 11-8 shift
• Monday - Thursday 9-6, and Friday 8-5
• Monday - Thursday 9-7, and Friday 8-12
• Displays proficiency in the following global competencies:
• Initiative – the understanding of what action needs to be taken and completing these tasks while seeking the perspective of others.
• Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility) – taking personal responsibility for mistakes; does what he/she commits to and is honest and forthright.
• Collaboration – works as a team player to make a difference within the business and expresses gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance, and support
• Willingness to Learn – handles change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and is open minded and curious to face new challenges.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
• 5% Travel Requirement (1% - 3% Overseas)
• Must be able to lift 50 pounds
• Must be able to sit and/or stand for extended periods of time (2-4 hours at a time)
• Must be able to work on a computer for extended periods of time (up to 4 hours at a time)
• Must be able to travel in vehicles (automobiles, trains, and planes) for up to 10 hours
• Must be able to communicate effectively in verbal and written English
• Must comply with company dress code guidelines
• Punctuality and attendance in accordance with company policies
• Timely reporting of expenses

SYNOPSIS: 
The Field Application Engineer (FAE) will provide technical, hands-on expertise to support all business opportunities for Phoenix Contact products and applications directly associated with the automation, control and networking portfolio used within the industrial automation and electronics industry. The Field Application Engineer will collaborate with regional sales managers, strategic account managers, sales engineers, account development specialists, channel, and field application engineering teams, to utilize a consultative approach with all customers to support their individual needs and expand market penetration. This role plays a pivotal part in the development and expansion of all accounts, through automation, control and networking sales growth and specifications.  This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities.  

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Increase annual sales revenue across all products directly associated with the automation, control, and networking portfolio at customer accounts. These products include but are not limited to ethernet products (wired or wireless), safety, controllers, IO, HMIs, IPCs and engineered solutions.
•	Work in coordination with local Phoenix Contact sales team utilizing joint sales activities, trainings, and mutual action plans to advance the sales of above-mentioned products.
•	Act as the local technical representative for the above-mentioned products and support the sales team in power-related technical matters.
•	Act as the regional marketing specialist developing sales tools, collateral and driving campaigns.  
•	Serve as the “voice of the customer” providing insight for expansion of product lines or new focus industries.
•	Drive value-added applications and engineered solutions. 
•	Meet/exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President - Field Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Develops and maintains strong relationships with potential clients, partners, and other stakeholders to facilitate new business and sustain growth. 
•	Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Effectively communicate with the entire Phoenix Contact team, both verbally and written.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Active involvement in industry-related associations.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within area of focus.
•	Provide reports to RSM as required.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact automation, control and networking products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact automation, control, and networking products/solutions.
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year college degree.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent industry experience.
•	3 years minimum sales or marketing experience with automation and industrial control products.
•	Ability to work independently from home office and communicate effectively with colleagues with limited supervision.
•	Excellent oral/written communication and presentation skills.
•	Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 40%+ or as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

SYNOPSIS: The Industry Manager – Integrated Energy (Energy Resilience) is responsible for developing and advancing Application Engineering & Projects (AEP) opportunities across the U.S. market, with a focus on energy resilience and optimization in enterprise- and campus-scale environments. This role identifies complex customer challenges, translates them into structured and commercially viable project opportunities, and works across sales, engineering, and operational teams to position solutions that align with AEP capabilities and customer priorities.  This position is an individual contributor role and does not include formal people management responsibilities. This role does not have formal people management responsibilities.  The title “Manager” reflects responsibility for managing processes, programs, or strategic initiatives, not people.  Individual Contributor Managers are often subject matter experts who lead cross-functional efforts, influence decisions, and own outcomes, but do not supervise direct reports.

	COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership
•	Core Competencies: Technical and commercial integration, Education and experience in system and project selling, System-level thinking and problem solving, Influence without authority, Opportunity structuring and solution framing, Strategic thinking and market insight, Strong stakeholder-facing skills, including executive presentation, consultative selling, workshop facilitation, and business case development.
RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Lead the development of the Integrated Energy (Energy Resilience) domain across the United States.
•	Identify and prioritize target markets, including enterprise, campus, infrastructure, and government environments.
•	Assess fragmented energy ecosystems to uncover opportunities for optimization, resilience, and modernization.
•	Contribute market insight and domain expertise to national AEP strategy.
•	Build and maintain a national pipeline of technically viable project opportunities in partnership with sales teams.
•	Identify inefficiencies and unmet needs across customer energy systems and operations.
•	Advance opportunities that can be translated into executable AEP projects.
•	Translate customer challenges into structured energy optimization and resilience concepts.
•	Partner with Application Engineering teams to define practical, technically sound solutions.
•	Develop integrated approaches that improve efficiency, system performance, and energy resilience.
•	Technical domains include microgrids, distributed energy resources (DERs), controls, central plants, combined heat, and power (CHP), solar plus storage, demand response, resilience planning, and grid interconnection.
•	Convert qualified opportunities into clearly defined project scopes.
•	Support roadmap development, feasibility assessment, and scope definition.
•	Contribute to commercial framing, value articulation, and business case development.
•	Engage energy managers, operations leaders, and infrastructure stakeholders to understand customer priorities and constraints.
•	Guide customers toward practical, high-value energy programs and project pathways.
•	Navigate complex stakeholder environments to build alignment and momentum around opportunity development.
•	Serve as a key interface among sales, engineering, operations, and other internal stakeholders.
•	Drive alignment and decision-making without direct authority.
•	Lead opportunity development through influence, coordination, and clear communication.
•	Ensure a structured transition of qualified opportunities into project execution.
•	Maintain involvement through early execution to support continuity and successful handoff.
•	Provide feedback and insights that strengthen AEP processes, offerings, and go-to-market effectiveness.
•	Serve as a subject matter expert in energy resilience.
•	Help shape strategy, solution positioning, and capability development within the domain.
•	Support internal teams with market, customer, and technical insight.
•	Other duties as assigned by manager.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree in engineering, energy systems, HVAC/MEP, or a related field.
•	7–10+ years of experience in energy resilience, engineering, technical business development, or a closely related field.
•	Demonstrated ability to translate technical challenges into structured project opportunities.
•	Working knowledge of integrated energy systems, energy optimization, and resilience-focused solution development.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Experience with the Resource Efficiency Manager (REM) program or equivalent models.
•	Experience in government, institutional, campus-scale, or complex infrastructure environments.
•	Proven success delivering energy optimization, efficiency, or resilience-focused initiatives.
•	Experience shaping and developing opportunities, not solely closing pre-defined solutions.

MEASURES OF SUCCESS: 
•	Development of a strong national pipeline of qualified, executable AEP opportunities.
•	Conversion of qualified opportunities into executable AEP projects.
•	Effective cross-functional alignment throughout opportunity development and transition.
•	Expansion of AEP opportunities within enterprise, campus, and infrastructure accounts.
ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel by air
•	Occasional overseas travel for extended periods required
•	Occasional Domestic Travel required
•	Must be able to sit for extended periods of time
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in English
•	Must be able to lift 25 lbs.
•	Regular and on-time attendance. 
•

SYNOPSIS:
The Mold Set Up Technician is responsible for set up, heat up, start up, material handling, operating, quality control, troubleshooting, changeovers and adjusting process settings for plastic injection molding presses and related equipment per set up sheet in the plant.

Ongoing training is required in this role and will include:  Arburg machines, Wittmann central material preparation, optical measuring system, quality requirements, Hydra Software and other training as determined.  Off-site training and travel may also be required on occasion.


RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Responsible for set up, heat up, start up, operating, troubleshooting, and adjusting process settings for 
        plastic injection molding presses and related equipment per set up sheet in the plant.
•       Demonstrate problem solving and strong troubleshooting skills supporting injection molding process.
•	Train and assist shift personnel, machine operator, etc. 
•	Assist maintenance with troubleshooting and equipment repair as directed.
•	Provide analysis, spreadsheets related to molds and injection molding processes.
•	Experience in mold care, repair, installation & removal of molds.
•	Ability to perform cavity/core changeovers, maintenance of all mold tooling in the department.
•	Perform preventive maintenance on molding equipment (hopper, dryer, TCU, conveyor) and molds.
•	Ability to oversee, monitor and perform all operations for the entire plastics injection molding 
        department on his/hers shift.
•	Ensures safety and housekeeping standards are maintained.
•	Works on and feel safe working on and handling tools up to 2 tons.
•	Assists and trains others in job as needed.
•	Responsible for area cleanliness and organization according to workplace standards.
•	Performs related duties as assigned by supervisor.
•	Follows procedures associated with ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and OSHAS 18001.
•	Follows necessary PPE Requirements for a given work center.
QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Associate Degree in Mechanical/Electrical Technology is preferred.
•	5+ years experience with injection molding is preferred.
•       Injection molding troubleshooting skills is required.
•	Working knowledge of plastic injection molding process, plastics is required.
•	PC and Windows based software experience including Microsoft Office products.
•	Able to read and understand engineering drawings.
•	Ability to communicate and work with others effectively.
•	Ability to work independently and effectively perform with limited supervision.
•	Consistently demonstrate a positive attitude toward all aspects of work.
•	Remains adaptable and open to change.
•	Applies effort and energy to lifelong learning and strive to continually grow.
•	Embraces change if it will provide future benefits. 
ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Able to lift at least 50 pounds.
•	Able to use pallet jack with 2 ton load.
•	Able to use portable staircase and climb to 12 foot heights.
•	Able to carry 20 pound/ 3 cubic feet boxes up and down portable staircase.
•	Able to work a 6 day week, if required.
•	Able to stand or sit continually for 4 hours without break.
•	Able to use computer for two hours at a time without break.
•	Able to drive to local supplier, in own vehicle (with reimbursement), at short notice.
•	Able to travel to off-site locations, including other Phoenix global sites, for periods of up to 2 weeks.

SYNOPSIS: The Solutions Marketing Specialist is a hands-on, execution-focused role responsible for driving awareness, adoption, and commercial success of Phoenix Contact’s industrial networking, connectivity, and communications solutions across key application areas, including Transportation, Energy, and Public Safety.

This role combines solution marketing with integrated marketing execution, with a strong emphasis on digital content creation, website and CMS (Drupal) management, and campaign deployment. The Specialist develops and executes solution-based messaging, creates digital and visual assets, and manages marketing initiatives that directly support business growth.

As a proactive and self-directed contributor, this individual identifies gaps, builds new solutions and content, and drives initiatives forward—not just supporting existing programs. Working cross-functionally with Product Management, Sales, and Industry teams, the role ensures effective go-to-market execution and customer engagement across digital and traditional channels.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Support the development of a solutions-first marketing approach for industrial networking and communications technologies (e.g., switches, cellular, wireless connectivity) across Transportation, Energy, and Public Safety.
•	Identify and prioritize target applications and market segments within these ecosystems.
•	Translate product capabilities into clear, application-focused value messaging and customer-centric content.
•	Develop application-focused materials, including digital content, solution overviews, use cases, and case studies.
•	Own and manage website content, including product pages, solution pages, and landing pages, using CMS (Drupal); apply basic HTML/CSS as needed to ensure accuracy and optimization.
•	Support SEO and digital marketing initiatives through content optimization, campaign landing pages, performance tracking, and continuous improvement.
•	Develop and execute digital marketing campaigns, including email campaigns, lead-nurturing workflows, and marketing automation using Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot).
•	Create and produce digital and visual assets (e.g., graphics, presentations, videos, and campaign materials) using Adobe Creative Suite.
•	Support product positioning and the development of product launch and sales enablement materials, including product briefs, presentations, and brochures.
•	Drive execution of solution launches, campaigns, and go-to-market initiatives aligned with commercial objectives.
•	Identify gaps in marketing content, campaigns, or positioning and proactively develop solutions to address them.
•	Monitor campaign and digital performance metrics and recommend optimizations to improve engagement and outcomes.
•	Collaborate cross-functionally with Product Management, Sales, Engineering, and Marketing teams to align messaging with application and market needs.
•	Support planning and execution of trade shows and events, including booth coordination, event content, and integrated campaign support.
•	Manage multiple projects and timelines to ensure deliverables are completed on schedule.
•	Demonstrate initiative by independently driving work forward while incorporating input from stakeholders as needed.
•	Take ownership of responsibilities by following through on commitments and demonstrating accountability.
•	Other duties as assigned by manager.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field 
•	Experience in product marketing, sales enablement, or digital marketing within a B2B environment 
•	Ability to translate technical information into clear, concise, customer-focused messaging 
•	Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (e.g., Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Premiere Pro) for content and asset creation 
•	Experience with content management systems (preferably Drupal) and website administration 
•	Working knowledge of SEO, digital analytics, and campaign performance tracking 
•	Experience with marketing automation platforms (e.g., Pardot) preferred 
•	Familiarity with HTML and CSS preferred 
•	Familiarity with industrial networking, ethernet switches, cellular routers, and/or wireless communications (Wi-Fi/radio) at a high level preferred
•	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Word, Excel) 
•	Excellent written and verbal communication skills 
•	Demonstrated ability to work as a self-directed, proactive contributor, identifying needs and executing solutions independently 
•	Experience supporting industrial, technical, or application-driven markets (Transportation, Energy, Public Safety preferred)
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

SUPPORTING QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Exposure to Transportation, Energy, or Public Safety markets
•	Exposure to Networking Communications
•	Experience supporting product launches
•	Experience developing application-focused or solutions-based content
•	Familiarity with digital marketing channels (web, email, social)


ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	10–20% travel required.
•	Must be able to sit and/or stand for extended periods (2–4 hours at a time).
•	Must be able to work on a computer for extended periods (up to 4 hours at a time).
•	Must be able to travel via automobile, train, or plane for up to 10 hours.
•	Must be able to communicate effectively in verbal and written English.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.   
•	This is an in-person position unless otherwise specified requiring 5 days in the office. After a probationary period, there may be an opportunity for Hybrid work.

Together, we are creating a sustainable world based on our passion for technology and innovation - we are searching for people who share this purpose with us to join our International Fellowship Program based at our headquarters in Bloomberg, Germany. 
In this role you will join a 2-year master program in Information Technology at TH OWL University of Applied Sciences and Arts starting October 2024. During and between semesters, you will have chance to work on real life projects in our automation/interface research and development team from the very beginning while being supported on a guided development journey. Upon completion of the program, you will return to the US in a role within our automation/interface team. 

What you can expect
•	A fully funded 2 years full-time master study with a global industry leading company, including a scholarship, a sponsorship for the tuition fees and a paid working student contract throughout the master study.
•	Full benefit package available through our German subsidiary. 
•	2 years working experience abroad in Germany for a global acting company.
•	Full time role at Phoenix Contact US after graduation.
•	Praxis experience with selected business areas during and between semesters, together with a master thesis in cooperation with our company.
•	Support in helping you develop professionally, both hard and soft skills, through mentorship, workshops, on-the-job learning, etc.
•	Variety of networking events to get to know your peers and colleagues.
Requirements
•	Bachelor's degree or equivalent in Electrical Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Mechatronics, or another closely related field of study.
•	Minimum GPA of 3.0. 
•	Willingness to live, learn and work in Germany for at least two years.
•	Must meet Germany Student Visa eligibility requirements.
•	Keen interest in pursuing a career in Automation Development and passionate about offering solutions for business.
•	You work collaboratively, enjoy working in an environment of friendliness and honesty.
•	You are self-driven, adaptive to change, and think analytically.
•	You are ready to being engaged in both student and working life in an intercultural context.
•	Working experience in a relevant area is preferable.
•	Knowledge in German language is recommended.

Product Marketing Intern 

Synopsis
As the Product Marketing Intern, you will be responsible for supporting the product and marketing teams by assisting in product positioning in identified markets and creating marketing strategies and content for promoting the product solutions. This is not your average internship. You will play an integral part in bringing our Ethernet solutions to interesting applications you may not have known existed.
 
Responsibilities
•	Gather market research and competitive analysis to assist Product Management initiatives 
•	Assist with constructing product marketing campaigns and content for product promotional literature
•	Assist in the design and development of content for product manuals, data sheets, brochures, etc. in both print and web-based formats
•	Assist with product training content 
•	Coordinate and drive initiatives to enhance the user interfaces and experiences across our brand, for both product and content
•	Coordinate with marketing in finalizing marketing, technical, promotional and PR material required for product launches
•	Contribute to team effort by accomplishing special projects as needed. 

Requirements
•	Actively enrolled in a four-year college/university
•	Self-starter with a record of accomplishment and a passion for learning 
•	Ability to manage multiple complex projects
•	Technical aptitude with a creative mind
•	Experience with Photoshop, Illustrator & InDesign is a plus
•	Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively in a team
•	Strong verbal and written communication skills 
•	Understanding of communication technology preferred but not required

Internship Benefits
•	Able to prove your Product and Marketing skills with relevant job experience
•	Gain experience in the Internet of Things, very popular with Tech companies
•	Apply Technology and Creative Expertise with Real world customer applications
•	Be a part of next generation communication infrastructure
•	Earn a fair wage while learning
•	Perfect first step toward a Product Manager, Product Marketing or Project Manager
•	Have Fun (Required)

About EtherWAN Systems, Inc
You see EtherWAN every day and probably don’t know it.  We can be found at almost every intersection in the silver cabinet that controls the traffic lights.  EtherWAN connects all these intersections to help improve the flow of traffic.  This will become hugely important once 5G and autonomous vehicles go mainstream.  EtherWAN’s core focus is on Ethernet connectivity for extreme environments with stringent product reliability requirements. EtherWAN’s product development, engineering, manufacturing and quality assurance processes are structured to push the limits of stated specifications resulting in products that continually exceed expectations.

SYNOPSIS:
This role is an entry-level position in the Development and Manufacturing (D&M) organization in manufacturing departments, offering a dual opportunity as a machine operator and an apprentice. The apprentice will gain exposure to key manufacturing processes through a department rotation program. The position includes 8,000 hours of structured education and on-the-job training, leading to a Mechatronics Technician certification from the United States Department of Labor along with an Associate Degree in Mechatronics from an accredited college.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate, Deliver Results, Take Ownership, Drive Customer Value, Develop Yourself, Improve and Innovate
•	General Competencies: Adaptability; Communication; Customer Orientation; Analytic Thinking; Diagnostic Information Gathering; Innovation; Thoroughness
•	Technical Competencies: Industrial Safety; Technical Documentation; Machine Installation; Maintenance and Repair; PLC Programming; Robotic Systems

RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Acquire knowledge of the technical aspects of Phoenix Contact products to understand production methods and resources.
•	Operate various equipment and machinery in the manufacturing area, transitioning from a line operator to a Mechatronics Technician.
•	Monitor and analyze equipment performance to optimize efficiency.
•	Assist Mechatronics Technicians in troubleshooting, maintaining, repairing, and interpreting mechanical and electrical aspects of automatic and semi-automatic equipment.
•	Comprehend and control quality standards, revisions, and non-conformances within camera programs and software.
•	Review machine enhancements, improvements, and developments with the Phoenix Contact machine build and Manufacturing Engineering department for better understanding.
•	Design basic mechanical tooling drawings in AutoCAD when required.
•	Learn to identify controller and I/O faults, diagnose causes, identify defective parts, debug control programs, and restore system function promptly.
•	Maintain a spare parts list and system to support equipment.
•	Participate in preventative maintenance programs for all automatic and semi-automatic equipment.
•	Provide technical support to machine operators as needed.
•	Adhere to necessary PPE requirements, appropriate ISO specifications,
•	Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Possess a High School Diploma, Vocational School certificate, or a 2-year associate degree, each with a GPA of 2.5 or higher.
•	Have studied engineering or industrial technology in high school or technical school, with knowledge of manufacturing, assembly, and print reading.
•	Attend technical training classes at local colleges and technical training centers as required by the Mechatronics Technician Program to fulfill educational mandates.
•	Express willingness to pursue an Associate Degree in Mechatronics.
•	Have experience in AutoCAD or other computer-aided design software (preferred).
•	Possess experience with PC and Windows-based software.
•	Demonstrate the ability to communicate and work with others effectively.
•	Show the ability to work independently and perform effectively with limited supervision.
•	Have successfully completed an aptitude test.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Able to lift at least 50 pounds.
•	Able to use pallet jack with 300-pound load.
•	Able to use portable staircase and climb to 12-foot heights.
•	Able to carry 20 pound/ 3 cubic feet boxes up and down portable staircase.
•	Able to push 4-wheeled cart with 100-pound load.
•	Able to work a 6-day week, if required.
•	Able to stand or sit continually for four hours without break.
•	Able to travel to offsite locations including German Headquarters for periods of up to 3 to 5 weeks.
•	Able to use computer for fours at a time without break
•	Able to drive to local supplier, in own vehicle, at short notice.
•	Able to travel to offsite locations including German Headquarters for periods of up to 3 to 5 weeks
•	Must be able to speak and understand English to communicate verbally and in writing

SYNOPSIS:
Contribute to production performance and training, in addition to equipment management and implementation. Working with engineering and quality, supports the production team in achieving established targets and assures customer satisfaction. The Mechatronics Technician II will modify, maintain, and repair electrical, electronic, and mechanical components, equipment, and systems to ensure proper functioning. The Mechatronics Technician II represents the second level within the Mechatronics Technician career path.

COMPETENCY PROFILE:
• Global Competencies: Initiative; Comprehensive Thinking (Personal Credibility); Collaboration; Willingness to learn
• General Competencies: Adaptability; Communication; Customer Orientation; Analytic Thinking; Diagnostic Information Gathering; Innovation; Thoroughness
• Technical Competencies: Industrial Safety; Technical Documentation; Machine Installation; Maintenance and Repair; PLC Programming; Robotic Systems

RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Supports technical production performance, training, and resources.
• Monitors and analyzes equipment performance and optimizes machine performance for efficient production.
• Troubleshoots, maintains, repairs, and interprets various mechanical and electrical aspects of automatic and semi-automatic equipment, such as mechanical and pneumatic, PLC, and PC-based driven systems.
• Understands quality standards and controls quality revisions and non-conformances.
• Coordinates machine enhancements, improvements, and development with machine build and Manufacturing Engineering department.
• Designs mechanical tooling drawings when required in AutoCAD and coordinates purchases from local tooling suppliers.
• Completes machine qualification testing and documentation for new equipment.
• Establishes and maintains spare parts list and spare parts system to support equipment.
• Establishes and manages preventative maintenance programs on all automatic and semi-automatic equipment.
• Works closely with all manufacturing personnel and provides technical support.
• Understands department processes, including material flow, planning initiatives, and strategy. Recommends improvements for material disposition and order sequencing.
• Follows necessary PPE requirements, appropriate ISO specifications and company procedures.
• Utilizes quality tools and continuous improvement techniques to improve department targets.
• Other duties as assigned.

QUALIFICATIONS:
• Experience in a production environment.
• Experience with AutoCAD or other computer aided design program(s).
• Knowledge of Mechanical assembly.
• Knowledge of industry standards.
• Demonstrate initiative by understanding what actions need to be taken and completing the required tasks while seeking the perspective of others as required.
• Demonstrate an honest and forthright presence by taking responsibility for own mistakes and not blaming others and by doing what he / she commits to doing.
• Work as a team player to make a difference within the business and express gratitude and appreciation to others who have provided information, assistance or support.
• Readily handle and embrace change through taking advantage of lifelong learning opportunities and being open minded and curious in the face of new challenges.

SUPPORTING QUALIFICATIONS:
• Associate Degree in Mechatronics, Engineering Technology, or another related field and 2 – 5 years of relevant work experience; Or 4 – 7 years of relevant work experience.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
• Able to lift at least 50 pounds.
• Able to use pallet jack with 300-pound load.
• Able to use portable staircase and climb to 12-foot heights.
• Able to carry 20 pound/ 3 cubic feet boxes up and down portable staircase.
• Able to push 4-wheeled cart with 100-pound load.
• Able to work a 6-day week, if required.
• Able to stand or sit continually for four hours without break.
• Able to travel to offsite locations including German Headquarters for periods of up to 2 to 3 weeks.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS: 
The Product Manager is responsible for the strategic planning, development, and lifecycle management of Phoenix Contact’s North American circuit breaker product portfolio. This portfolio is comprised of thermal-magnetic and hydraulic circuit breakers. This role ensures that all product initiatives are aligned with Phoenix Contact’s overall business strategy and market positioning within North America. The Product Manager leads the creation and execution of the product roadmap, working cross-functionally to define product requirements and drive go-to-market strategies. They are expected to balance customer needs, technological capabilities, and business objectives to deliver innovative, high-impact solutions. Key responsibilities include defining and evolving the product vision, managing the full product lifecycle from ideation through end-of-life, conducting market and competitive analysis, and collaborating with engineering, marketing, sales, and support teams to ensure successful product development and launch. The Product Manager also monitors product performance and customer feedback to drive continuous improvement, ensuring Phoenix Contact’s North American circuit breaker portfolio remains competitive and aligned with market demands.

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership
•	General Competencies: Customer Orientation; Analytical Thinking & Business Acumen
•	Technical Competencies: Technical Expertise; Strategic Thinking; Fostering Innovation; Personal Effectiveness; Managing Performance

RESPONSIBILITES:
•	Be the subject matter expert for circuit breaker technology – i.e., trip curves, short circuit current ratings, etc.
•	Gather market research, customer feedback and stakeholder input to arrive at a strategic product plan 
•	Define what products are suitable for target markets.
•	Develop and maintain product strategy and roadmap, and communicate strategy to appropriate internal stakeholders 
•	Manage the entire lifecycle of the product line including product concept, design, sample production, testing, forecast, cost, mass production, spec/feature additions and modifications, and product end of life. 
•	Ensure quality of the product line through collaboration with QA and factory. 
•	Set prioritization and work with product development team in setting product release deadlines
•	Write functional specifications, customer value propositions statements, and expected benefit or loss (if not implemented) of a product or application concept.
•	Ongoing communication on product’s progress to executives, sales team, and other stakeholders. 
•	Coordinate with the Product Marketing department to finalize marketing, technical, promotional, and PR material required for product introductions. 
•	Develop training material and provide training to sales and technical teams. 
•	Travel and participate in market activities such as standards meeting, specialty trade shows, conferences, key customer visits, and forums to gain closer access to customer and industry trends. 
•	Up to 20% travel, including international travel.   
•	Perform other work-related duties as assigned 
QUALIFICATIONS:
·	Bachelor’s degree in engineering discipline or equivalent work experience is required.
·	3+ years of working knowledge of product development and/or product marketing experience with circuit breaker technology
·	Experience with networking technologies including cellular and ethernet highly desired
·	Candidate must be well organized, self-motivated, and capable of seeing projects through to completion. 
·	High level of accountability and planning capabilities, communicates with transparency and integrity. 
·	Strong written and verbal communication skills are required. 
·	Proven ability to influence cross-functional teams.
·	Successfully collaborate in a dynamic and diverse environment. 
·	Proficient with Salesforce, Microsoft Visio, & MS Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
·	Experience in vertical markets such as material handling, industrial machinery, power generation & energy, or data centers is a plus. 
·	Able to travel 20%
·	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
·	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
·	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
·	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
·	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
·	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward.


ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane for up to 14 hours
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time
•	Must be able to travel overnight 
•	Must be able to sit for 4 hours at a time
•	Must be able to stand for extended periods of time
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs. 
•	Regular and on-time attendance. 
•	This is a hybrid position requiring at least three days in the office, unless otherwise specified.

SYNOPSIS:
The Product Marketing Specialist – plays a pivotal role in driving awareness, adoption, and sales enablement of Phoenix Contact’s industrial, low-wattage DIN Rail power supplies. This individual contributor bridges technical expertise and marketing strategy by supporting product launches, training, and promotional activities across internal teams, distributors, and customers. The role also contributes to the coordination and execution of marketing plans, advertising campaigns, tradeshows, and technical support initiatives. Reporting to the Director of Product – Power Reliability, the Specialist collaborates closely with internal stakeholders, sales teams, and distribution partners to advance network product solutions and ensure successful market engagement.

COMPETENCY PROFILE: 
Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership

JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:

•	Deliver product training to internal teams, distributors, and customers, focusing on product features, benefits, and applications

metadata.value:
SYNOPSIS:  The Sales Engineer is responsible for driving sales of technical and industrial products by combining engineering knowledge with strong interpersonal and business skills. This role involves identifying customer needs, recommending suitable products or solutions, and providing technical support throughout the sales process. The engineer works closely with manufacturing, R&D, and marketing teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets.  This role play a pivotal in the marketing, sales, customer service and business development required to promote and support the organization’s products.  Works to improve the organization’s market position and achieve financial growth. 

COMPETENCY PROFILE:  
•	Global Competencies: Collaborate; Deliver Results; Develop Yourself; Drive Customer Value; Improve and Innovate; Take Ownership
	
	RESPONSIBILITIES:
•	Meet/exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Director of Sales.
•	Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
•	Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
•	Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 16 to 20 sales calls per week, with at least 50% to assigned accounts.
•	Fully utilize Avenue as a communication, reporting, measurement and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
•	Effectively communicate with the entire Phoenix Contact team, both verbally and written.
•	Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
•	Consistently provide feedback to the field and Harrisburg regarding territory requirements as it relates to personnel, product, and/or market requirements.
•	Work closely with Harrisburg Marketing to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
•	Active involvement in industry related associations.
•	Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
•	Continue personal development through an effective skill building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
•	Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within area of focus.
•	Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
•	Develop and implement effective sales plans across a geographic territory as defined by regional management.
•	Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned distributor locations.
•	Work independently or in conjunction with regional management to plan, document, measure and grow assigned distributor locations.
•	Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
•	Where applicable, work with National Accounts Team and Harrisburg Marketing to implement global/national account strategies in order to achieve program objectives.
•	Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
•	Qualify and follow-up on all leads within assigned territory and/or account portfolio.
•	Follow-up on all quotations within assigned territory and/or account portfolio.
•	Responsible for all sales related activities within assigned territory, with a focus on all control cabinet, machine mounted, and industrial networking solutions.
•	Drive value-added/custom solutions within assigned account base, comprised primarily of industrial OEMs, local end users and project business.
•	Become proficient on all control cabinet, machine mounted and industrial networking applications and our corresponding solutions, to the point of being considered a consultant by the market.
•	Other duties as assigned.
	
	QUALIFICATIONS:
•	Four-year degree.
•	Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent industrial industry experience.
•	3 to 5 years’ minimum sales experience in the electronics marketplace.
•	Experience working with distribution preferred.
•	Demonstrates a willingness to collaborate with others, build relationships, and involve others in decision making. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to deliver results by solving or supporting others in solving problems, prioritizing tasks, and/or resources, and focusing on outcomes. 
•	Demonstrates self-awareness and the willingness to learn and grow, accept changes, and effectively adapt to changing work demands. 
•	Demonstrates the ability to drive customer value through knowledge of the business, putting effort into identifying customer needs, and delivering work to maximize the value for customers. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to promote the value of improvement and innovation by staying ahead of trends, creating ideas and opportunities, and generating speed. 
•	Demonstrates a willingness to take ownership by making decisions, taking responsibility, and driving things forward. 

	ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:
•	Must be able to travel via airplane.
•	Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
•	Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
•	Must be able to travel overnight 20-25% as dictated by assigned territory.
•	Comply with company dress code guidelines.
•	Punctuality is required.
•	Regular and on-time attendance.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Summary

This is a hands-on, activity-driven role ideal for someone who is eager to learn, contribute, and grow within a dynamic team responsible for driving sales of all Phoenix Contact products by combining engineering knowledge with strong interpersonal and business skills. The Sales Engineer (OEM) plays a key role in identifying growth opportunities, building relationships with potential and existing developing customers, and supporting the sales pipeline through consistent outreach and follow-up. This role involves identifying customer needs, recommending suitable products or solutions, and providing technical support throughout the sales process. The Sales Engineer (OEM) works closely with our Channel and Field Application Engineering teams to ensure customer satisfaction and achieve sales targets. This role focuses on deepening relationships within assigned accounts, driving growth opportunities, and expanding business engagement to maximize long-term value.


Responsibilities

Meet or exceed territory sales quotas as established by the RSM and/or Vice President—Field Sales.
Be proficient in BASE selling skills and implement to build strong customer relationships.
Generate new opportunities within assigned accounts through ongoing communication, relationship development, and collaboration with key stakeholders.
Support the preparation of proposals, presentations, and sales materials.
Maintain and update CRM systems with accurate client information. Fully utilize the CRM as a communication, reporting, measurement, and tracking tool to enhance productivity.
Collaborate with the entire Phoenix Contact team to align efforts, effectively communicate both verbally and written.
Track and report on business development activities and performance metrics.
Differentiate Phoenix Contact from other component vendors by building “partner” relationships with customers.
Focus on the customer by averaging a minimum of 16 to 20 sales calls per week.
Be proficient in and drive the use of all sales tools provided to support local markets.
Work closely with Harrisburg Product teams to aid in the development of field presentations and application notes.
Proactively utilize all resources and sales tools to maximize productivity, responsiveness, and customer delight.
Continue personal development through an effective skill-building program as determined by the RSM, Director of Sales, and/or the Harrisburg Training Department.
Demonstrate technical competence on all products/solutions within the area of focus.
Provide forecasts and reports to RSM as required.
Independently and/or in conjunction with regional management, develop and implement effective Mutual Action Plans with assigned customer locations.
Deliver presentations and hands-on demonstrations of Phoenix Contact products, applications, and technology for the purpose of product sales.
Develop and maintain a working knowledge on all Phoenix Contact products/solutions.
Qualify and follow up on all opportunities within the assigned account portfolio.
Follow up on all quotations within the assigned account portfolio.
Qualifications

Four-year degree preferred.
Technical discipline preferred and/or equivalent experience.
1 to 3 years minimum sales experience in the electronics or industrial automation & controls marketplace.
Experience working with distribution preferred.
Comfortable conducting regular outreach and check-ins with assigned accounts to maintain engagement and uncover new opportunities.
Self-motivated with the ability to learn and adapt quickly.
Essential Job Functions

Must be able to travel via airplane.
Must have a valid driver’s license and be able to travel via automobile for up to six hours at a time.
Must be able to lift a minimum of 50 lbs.
Must be able to travel overnight 40% as dictated by assigned territory.
Comply with company dress code guidelines.
Punctuality is required.
Regular and on-time attendance.
